volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 3.3.605 - Section 4.1.659b)

sections.label: Section 3.3.605
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.605b
sections.original: Cliniam orat sibi uti id nunc det ; illam illi tamen
sections.translation: She is soliciting Clinia at once to advance her this money ; she says , however , that this girl is to be a security , that , at a future time , she will repay the thousand pieces of money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.606b
sections.translation: And would she really be a security ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.606c
sections.translation: Dear me , is it to be doubted ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.607b
sections.original: Ego sic putavi : quid nunc facere cogitas ?
sections.translation: What then do you intend doing ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.608
sections.original: Egone ? ad Menedemum ibo : dicam hanc esse captam ex Caria
sections.translation: What , I ? I shall go to Menedemus ; I ’ ll tell him she is a captive from Caria , rich , and of noble family ; if he redeems her , there will be a considerable profit in this transaction .

sections.label: Section 3.3.610
sections.translation: You are in an error .

sections.label: Section 3.3.610b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.610c
sections.original: Pro Menedemo nunc tibi ego respondeo :
sections.translation: I ’ ll now answer you for Menedemus — I will not purchase her .

sections.label: Section 3.3.611b
sections.translation: What is it you say ? Do speak more agreeably to our wishes .

sections.label: Section 3.3.611c
sections.translation: But there is no occasion .

sections.label: Section 3.3.612
sections.translation: No occasion ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.612b
sections.translation: Certainly not , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 3.3.612c
sections.translation: How so , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.612d
sections.translation: You shall soon know . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.613
sections.original: Mane , mane : quid est quod tam a nobis graviter crepuerunt fores ?
sections.translation: Stop , stop ; what is the reason that there is such a great noise at our door ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.614
sections.original: Nisi me animus fallit , hic profecto est annulus quem ego suspicor
sections.translation: Unless my fancy deceives me , surely this is the ring which I suspect it to be , the same with which my daughter was exposed .

sections.label: Section 4.1.615b
sections.original: Quid volt sibi , Syre , haec oratio ?
sections.translation: Syrus , what is the meaning of these expressions ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.616
sections.translation: Nurse , how is it ? Does it not seem to you the same ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.616b
sections.original: Dixi equidem ubi mihi ostendisti ilico
sections.translation: As for me , I said it was the same the very instant that you showed it me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.617b
sections.original: At ut satis contemplata modo sis , mea nutrix .
sections.translation: But have you now examined it thoroughly , my dear nurse ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.617c
sections.translation: Thoroughly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.618
sections.original: Abi iam nunc intro , atque illa si iam laverit mihi nuntia :
sections.translation: Then go in-doors at once , and if she has now done bathing , bring me word . I ’ ll wait here in the mean time for my husband .

sections.label: Section 4.1.619b
sections.translation: She wants you , see what it is she wants ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.620
sections.original: Nescio quid tristis est : non temere est : metuo quid sit .
sections.translation: she is in a serious mood , I don ’ t know why ; it is not without a cause — I fear what it may be .

sections.label: Section 4.1.620b
sections.translation: What it may be ? I ’ faith , she ’ ll now surely be announcing some important trifle , with a great parade .

sections.label: Section 4.1.622
sections.translation: Ha ! my husband !

sections.label: Section 4.1.622b
sections.translation: Ha ! my wife !

sections.label: Section 4.1.622c
sections.translation: I was looking for you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.622d
sections.translation: Tell me what you want .

sections.label: Section 4.1.623
sections.original: Primum hoc te oro ne quid credas me adversum edictum tuum
sections.translation: In the first place , this I beg of you , not to believe that I have ventured to do any thing contrary to your commands .

sections.label: Section 4.1.624b
sections.original: Vin me istuc tibi , etsi incredibile est , credere ?
sections.translation: Would you have me believe you in this , although so incredible ? Well , I will believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.625b
sections.original: Nescio quid peccati portet haec purgatio .
sections.translation: This excuse portends I know not what offense .

sections.label: Section 4.1.626
sections.original: Meministin me esse gravidam , et mihi te maximopere dicere ,
sections.translation: Do you remember me being pregnant , and yourself declaring to me , most peremptorily , that if I should bring forth a girl , you would not have it brought up .

sections.label: Section 4.1.627b
sections.translation: I know what you have done , you have brought it up .

sections.label: Section 4.1.628b
sections.original: Sic est factum , domina , ergo herus damno auctus est .
sections.translation: Such is the fact , I ’ m sure : my young master has gained a loss [*] in consequence .

sections.label: Section 4.1.629
sections.original: Minime : sed erat hic Corinthia anus haud impura : ei dedi
sections.translation: Not at all ; but there was here an elderly woman of Corinth , of no indifferent character ; to her I gave it

sections.label: Section 4.1.630
sections.translation: to be exposed .

sections.label: Section 4.1.630b
sections.original: O Iupiter , tantamne esse in animo inscitiam !
sections.translation: O Jupiter ! that there should be such extreme folly in a person ’ s mind .

sections.label: Section 4.1.631
sections.translation: Alas ! what have I done ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.631b
sections.translation: And do you ask the question ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.631c
sections.translation: If I have acted wrong , my dear Chremes , I have done so in ignorance .

sections.label: Section 4.1.632b
sections.original: Id equidem ego si tu neges , certo scio
sections.translation: This , indeed , I know for certain , even if you were to deny it , that in every thing you both speak and act ignorantly and foolishly : how many blunders you disclose in this single affair ! For , in the first place , then , if

sections.label: Section 4.1.635
sections.translation: you had been disposed to obey my orders , the child ought to have been dispatched ; you ought not in words to have feigned her death , and in reality to have left hopes of her surviving . But that I pass over ; compassion , maternal affection , I allow it . But how finely you did provide for the future ! What was your meaning ? Do reflect . It ’ s clear , beyond a doubt , that your daughter was betrayed by you to this old woman ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.640
sections.original: Per te vel uti quaestum faceret , vel uti veniret palam .
sections.translation: either that through you she might make a living by her , or that she might be sold in open market as a slave . I suppose you reasoned thus : any thing is enough , if only her life is saved : what are you to do with those who understand neither law , nor right and justice ? Be it for better or for worse , be it for them or against them , they see nothing except just what they please .

sections.label: Section 4.1.644
sections.original: Mi Chreme , peccavi , fateor : vincor : nunc hoc te obsecro ,
sections.translation: My dear Chremes , I have done wrong , I own ; I am convinced . Now this I beg of you ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.645
sections.original: Quanto tuus est animus natu gravior , ignoscentior ,
sections.translation: inasmuch as you are more advanced in years than I , be so much the more ready to forgive ; so that your justice may be some protection for my weakness .

sections.label: Section 4.1.647
sections.original: Scilicet equidem istuc factum ignoscam : verum , Sostrata ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll readily forgive you doing this , of course ; but , Sostrata , my easy temper prompts you to do amiss . But , whatever this circumstance is , by reason of which this was begun upon , proceed to tell it .

sections.label: Section 4.1.649b
sections.original: Ut stultae et misere omnes sumus
sections.translation: As we women are all foolishly and wretchedly

sections.label: Section 4.1.650
sections.original: Religiosae , quum exponendam do illi , de digito annulum
sections.translation: superstitious , when I delivered the child to her to be exposed , I drew a ring from off my finger , and ordered her to expose it , together with the child ; that if she should die , she might not be without [*] some portion of our possessions .

sections.label: Section 4.1.653
sections.original: Istuc recte : conservasti te atque illam .
sections.translation: That was right ; thereby you proved the saving of yourself and her . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.1.653b
sections.translation: This is that ring .

sections.label: Section 4.1.654
sections.translation: Whence did you get it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.654b
sections.translation: From the young woman whom Bacchis brought here with her .

sections.label: Section 4.1.654c
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 4.1.655
sections.translation: What does she say ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.655b
sections.original: Ea lavatum dum it servandum mihi dedit .
sections.translation: She gave it me to keep for her , while she went to bathe . At first I paid no attention to it ; but after I looked at it , I at once recognized it , and came running to you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.657b
sections.translation: What do you suspect now , or have you discovered , relative to her ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.658b
sections.original: Nescio ; nisi ut ex ipsa quaeras unde hunc habuerit ,
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; unless you inquire of herself whence she got it , if that can possibly be discovered .

sections.label: Section 4.1.659b
sections.original: Interii : plus spei video quam volo .
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! I see more hopes [*] from this incident than I desire .
